title: [Bug]: PR label sync dry-run output should not claim labels were copied
summary: The pull request label synchronization helper can print copied-style summary text during dry runs, which is misleading in release evidence.
observed: Dry-run diagnostics can say that labels were copied even when no GitHub mutation was performed.
expected: Dry-run diagnostics should use future-tense wording such as would copy and should clearly distinguish planned operations from completed GitHub writes.
reproduction: Run python scripts/sync-pr-labels.py --dry-run for a PR with source issue labels.
failing_test: Add a unit test proving dry-run output uses would-copy wording and does not claim a completed mutation.
impact: Release evidence can become ambiguous. Runtime package behavior is unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to local GitHub workflow diagnostics and cannot affect message delivery semantics.
security: The fix must keep output sanitized and should not print payloads, secrets, private endpoints, or token values.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No public documentation update is required.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
